acceptance_targets: [CH13-BOOKER-01] In the revised passage spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:169-260, Eli's final route choice occurs after at least one explicit deadline pressure input and after he articulates one concrete loss attached to each option; if the choice could be unchanged by removing all incoming messages, this fails.

[CH13-CRAFT-01] For both target spans (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:85-90 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:371-372), the revised text must replace abstract explanatory lines with directly observable action/sensation and one character-specific thought tied to immediate stakes; if the beat can be summarized without losing concrete detail, this fails.

[CH13-CRAFT-02] In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:263-267, the rehab memory includes at least one specific recalled action or line and produces a clearly visible immediate behavioral shift before the next logistical step; if Eli can continue unchanged to the next task, this fails.

[CH13-DIALOGUE-01] In each revised exchange block (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:17-41 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:63-80), a blind read of any 6-line excerpt should allow speaker attribution by rhythm/register alone; if lines remain swappable without tags, this fails.

[CH13-PROSE-01] After revision of the target spans, no more than one 'smell/smelled like' template remains per scene and each target scene contains at least one non-olfactory sensory detail that carries the main descriptive load; otherwise fail.

[CH13-PROSE-02] In revised target lines, each of the three beats uses a different sentence pattern to show impulse control, and the exact 'before he could' construction appears no more than once across those beats; if two or more retain the same hinge, this fails.

[CH13-PROSE-03] Across revised target spans, no more than one character entrance uses the full descriptor stack; each of the other entrances must establish the character primarily through either action under pressure or distinctive speech rhythm within two sentences, else fail.

[FBK-02-007] PASS if revised lines around snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4246 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4334 deliver a clear, on-page pivot choice with immediate sacrifice; FAIL if the scene still reads as extended call-center transcript before choice.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passage spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:169-260, Eli's final route choice occurs after at least one explicit deadline pressure input and after he articulates one concrete loss attached to each option; if the choice could be unchanged by removing all incoming messages, this fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's stated objective (get east fast enough to affect deadlines) is undercut because Eli's decisive routing choice is functionally settled before live relational pressure enters the scene; the later Nora text confirms logistics but does not materially contest the choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the decision sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:169-181 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:247-260 so the east-vs-Reno choice happens under active pressure (e.g., deadline message/call arrives before purchase, or a direct consequence of delay is voiced before commitment). Keep the eventual Reno choice if desired, but make the tradeoff dramatic rather than merely arithmetic.

findings.acceptance_test: For both target spans (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:85-90 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:371-372), the revised text must replace abstract explanatory lines with directly observable action/sensation and one character-specific thought tied to immediate stakes; if the beat can be summarized without losing concrete detail, this fails.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at impact beats: the prose steps into abstract thematic summary (institutional boxes, better/worse chapter language) where the scene's pressure calls for embodied consequence and immediate psychological specificity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:85-90 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:371-372 with concrete, in-body rendering of Eli's collapse/control response (breath, posture, voice, impulse, self-accusation) instead of generalized commentary. Keep institutional power dynamics, but show them through moment-to-moment behavior.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:263-267, the rehab memory includes at least one specific recalled action or line and produces a clearly visible immediate behavioral shift before the next logistical step; if Eli can continue unchanged to the next task, this fails.
findings.problem: Timidity: the Theo rehab-abandonment memory is opened, then quickly evacuated into transaction logistics, retreating from the full emotional reality the premise establishes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand and deepen snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:263-267 so the memory beat holds long enough to change Eli's immediate behavior before he proceeds (e.g., failed first dial, visible shame response, altered wording in the next call). Avoid abstract guilt labels; use recalled action and present consequence.

findings.acceptance_test: In each revised exchange block (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:17-41 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:63-80), a blind read of any 6-line excerpt should allow speaker attribution by rhythm/register alone; if lines remain swappable without tags, this fails.
findings.problem: Institutional speakers blur into similar policy-script cadence and full-form diction, reducing idiolect separation and flattening leverage shifts in the impound/wire exchanges.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate voice texture across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:17-41, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:63-80, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:195-205 by giving each speaker a distinct rhythm: keep Brielle's mandated script where required, but give Renee and the counter clerk more situational colloquial texture and interruption under pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision of the target spans, no more than one 'smell/smelled like' template remains per scene and each target scene contains at least one non-olfactory sensory detail that carries the main descriptive load; otherwise fail.
findings.problem: Recurring sensory formula: repeated 'smell/smelled like' constructions appear across multiple scenes and begin to read as a default template rather than scene-specific perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:99-107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:247-249, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:387 to vary sensory channel and sentence architecture (e.g., sound/touch/spatial pressure carrying the beat instead of another smell comparison).

findings.acceptance_test: In revised target lines, each of the three beats uses a different sentence pattern to show impulse control, and the exact 'before he could' construction appears no more than once across those beats; if two or more retain the same hinge, this fails.
findings.problem: Recurring syntactic tic: the 'before he could ...' suppression structure appears repeatedly and creates mechanical cadence in moments that should feel distinct.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:243, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:311 with differentiated control-failure syntax (one interruption, one direct action, one external trigger) rather than repeating the same hinge phrase.

findings.acceptance_test: Across revised target spans, no more than one character entrance uses the full descriptor stack; each of the other entrances must establish the character primarily through either action under pressure or distinctive speech rhythm within two sentences, else fail.
findings.problem: Mechanical repetition from over-literal concreteness patterns: multiple side-character entrances use the same stacked template (clothing + small bodily wear marker + job prop), which flattens prose and reads generated.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework side-character entry beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:5-6, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:59-60, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:183-186, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:281-294 so introductions vary by leverage/action/dialogue rhythm rather than repeating descriptor stacks.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines around snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4246 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4334 deliver a clear, on-page pivot choice with immediate sacrifice; FAIL if the scene still reads as extended call-center transcript before choice.
findings.problem: Institutional call/paperwork sequence runs long in transcript mode, delaying the chapter's decisive pivot and muting embodied stakes despite high-pressure content.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten repetitive disclosure language at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4246, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4258, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4262, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4272; pull the send-now vs travel-now decision beat earlier and stage it physically before the depot accounting list at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4334.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally mixed behavior, bodily consequence, and economic detail when they are causally active. In edge cases, choose the more concrete and uncomfortable rendering unless it violates factual continuity; if two renderings are equally plausible, prefer the one that increases character accountability rather than reader reassurance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene contains pain, sex, addiction, debt panic, or humiliation, abstract phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with embodied specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, legal precision, or deliberate emotional distance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter architectures across the book: confrontation chapters, document-information turns, labor-process chapters, transit interiority chapters, and quiet relational chapters. Consecutive chapters must differ in scene density or dialogue-to-interiority ratio.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary American literary realism: precise, unsentimental, image-aware, and concrete under stress.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third-person anchored to Eli, with no unmarked POV drift.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Braided cadence: reflective medium-length sentences interrupted by blunt short lines at impact moments.
